value.HubSpot Status: ={{ $('Merge Data & Build Templates').item.json.hsContactFound ? 'Contact Updated' : 'New Contact Created' }}

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ JSON.stringify({ filterGroups: [{ filters: [{ propertyName: 'email', operator: 'EQ', value: $('Has Customer Email?1').first().json.customerEmail }] }], properties: ['firstname', 'lastname', 'email', 'company'], limit: 1 }) }}

parameters.content:
## Bug Resolution Notification System
This workflow works when a problem (bug) is marked as solved. It checks if the problem is really fixed and then informs the customer. It also updates company records and informs the team. If customer details are missing, it alerts the team for manual action.

### How It Works

	•	It detects when a problem is updated.
	•	It checks if the problem is a bug and marked as solved.
	•	It prepares all important information about the problem.
	•	It checks if this problem was already handled before.
	•	It checks if customer contact details are available.
	•	If details are available → sends update to customer.
	•	If details are missing → alerts team to handle manually.

### Setup Steps

	1.	Connect your bug tracking system.
	2.	Connect email service to send messages.
	3.	Connect customer management system.
	4.	Connect team communication tool.
	5.	Connect a sheet to store records.

## Step 1: Detect & Verify Bug Fix

	•	This step starts the process when a problem is updated. 
	•	It checks whether the issue is actually a bug and marked as solved. 
	•	If both conditions are true, the process continues.
	•	Otherwise, it stops here.

## Step 2: Prepare & Clean Information

	•	This step collects all details about the problem.
	•	It organizes information like name, priority, and description.
	•	It also fills missing details with default values.
	•	This makes the data easy to use in later steps.

## Step 3: Avoid Duplicate Work

	•	This step checks if the same problem was already processed before.
	•	If it was already handled, the workflow stops to avoid repetition.
	•	If not, it continues further.
	•	This helps prevent sending duplicate messages.

## Step 4: Check Customer Contact

	•	This step checks if customer email is available.
	•	If email exists, the process continues normally.
	•	If email is missing, it takes a different path.
	•	This ensures proper communication handling.

## Step 5: Collect Extra Details & Create Message

	•	This step gathers more information about the problem.
	•	It prepares a message to send to the customer.
	•	It also prepares notes for internal records.
	•	Everything is formatted in a clear and readable way.

## Step 6: Send Updates & Notify Everyone

	•	This step sends an email to the customer about the fix.
	•	It updates the problem with a note saying it is resolved.
	•	It updates customer records in the system.
	•	It also informs the team and saves the data in a sheet.

## Step 5: Handle Missing Customer Info

	•	If customer email is not available, this step runs.
	•	It logs the issue and marks it for manual follow-up.
	•	It sends an alert to the team.
	•	The team can then contact the customer manually.
